scope: Reference-first completion audit for the DeepSeek-V4-Flash AMD pretraining canary. This separates local PyTorch reference evidence, HF shape-contract evidence, short-shape parity, and missing HF numerical full-layer validation.

hf_flash_shape_contract.caveat: This proves the active Flash shape contract, not numerical parity against an HF model implementation or checkpoint.

default_full_preset.name: full
default_full_preset.read: preset_config('full') returns DSV4LayerConfig() and the dataclass default remains Pro-sized hidden=7168. Flash shape should use preset_config('flash') when available; older evidence may still record explicit HF contract overrides.

flash_shape_binding_for_local_runs.read: Flash-shape local runs are supported through the local flash preset when available; older retained evidence may still record the equivalent explicit HF contract overrides.

flash_preset_hf_config_consistency.read: Config-only oracle proves preset_config('flash') matches the HF Flash config fields. It does not run the full layer unless the optional --run-layer MI350 gate is executed.

flash_full_layer_local_oracle_smoke.read: Full Flash-dimension local PyTorch layer smoke passed on MI350 for FP32 and BF16 at B=1, S=1 with balanced routing and dense-mask attention. This proves a local clean-oracle forward/backward surface can execute with finite gradients; it is not HF numerical parity, S4096 performance evidence, or final sparse-MLA/LI production-kernel evidence.

flash_hyperconnection_full_layer_local_o.read: Full Flash-dimension Hyper-Connection local PyTorch layer smoke passed on MI350 for FP32 and BF16 at B=1, S=1. It exercises the rank-4 HC boundary around attention and MoE with input/output shape [1,1,4,4096]. This removes the local full-block HC smoke blocker, but it is not matched-weight HF numerical parity, S4096 coverage, or TorchTitan production-kernel evidence.

local_li_attention_and_layer_gradcheck.caveat: This is a tiny float64 CPU gradcheck gate. It is strong local autograd evidence for the reference path, but it is not full S4096 BF16/FP32 Flash numerical validation.

local_hyperconnection_reference_smoke.read: The local pure-PyTorch HC primitive now passes split/shape, small gradcheck, and Flash hidden-size FP32/BF16 backward smokes. The separate Flash HC full-layer oracle now also threads HC around the local attention and MoE block at B=1, S=1. Matched HF parity still requires an HF training adapter or matched HF-vs-local numerical harness.

promotion_blockers: module marks itself as a reference backend
Delegates to the Python/PyTorch manual_stream bridge.
Intended for ABI and parity checks only.
Not a fused ROCm kernel and not a performance backend.
module marks itself as a reference backend
Delegates to the Python/PyTorch manual_stream bridge.
Intended for ABI and parity checks only.
Not a fused ROCm kernel and not a performance backend.

sparse_mla_reference_backend_preflight.read: The manual_stream reference backend passes ABI smokes and supports backward without selected-state materialization, but it correctly fails production S4096 preflight because it is a reference/PyTorch bridge capped at seq_len 128.

shape.storage_dtype: torch.bfloat16 on CUDA, torch.float32 on CPU

ep_moe_backward_reference_contract.read: No MORI or AITER kernels are used here; this defines the exact VJP semantics the training-safe MORI+AITER custom autograd path must match.

mori_aiter_forward_plus_reference_backwa.read: training API semantics probe; routed mode is an intermediate contract, not the final fused MoE backward kernel

blockers: The configured Transformers tag does not expose a standard deepseek_v4 modeling/configuration module, so there is no drop-in HF training-layer parity class at that version.
HF's published repo source is inference-oriented and uses quantized runtime kernels; matched gradient parity still needs a BF16/FP32 training adapter or local HC oracle.
hf_architecture_parity_preflight.read: The source-contract preflight shows HF and Ascend use Hyper-Connections with rank-4 hidden state; the local clean reference now has an HC full-layer smoke, while matched-weight HF numerical parity remains incomplete. Matched HF numerical parity is still not persisted.

hf_numerical_full_layer_parity.required_evidence: A deterministic one-layer DeepSeek-V4-Flash HF-vs-local numerical parity run, ideally FP32 for oracle comparison and BF16 for training contract comparison, with matched config, weights, inputs, router/indexer controls, and explicit output and gradient tolerances.
hf_numerical_full_layer_parity.read: The current HF evidence is a config/shape contract plus an architecture preflight. No artifact found in run_artifacts proves local full-layer numerical parity against the HF implementation or checkpoint. The local HC full-layer oracle removes the local rank-4 boundary smoke blocker, but matched weights, inputs, router/indexer controls, and HF output/gradient tolerances still need to be recorded.

clean_pytorch_reference_full_layer_bf16_.reason: The local full-layer PyTorch harness and first-class Flash preset exist, the Flash preset matches the HF config contract, and full Flash-dimension B=1 S=1 MI350 local oracle smokes passed for FP32 and BF16. The newer oracle includes rank-4 HC around attention and MoE. This is still a local smoke, not deterministic HF matched-weight numerical parity or S4096 reference coverage.

validate_against_hf.reason: HF config was checked on 2026-05-31. The HF architecture preflight shows HF and Ascend use Hyper-Connections. The local clean reference now has both a primitive HC smoke and an HC full-layer B=1 S=1 MI350 smoke, but implementation/checkpoint-level HF matched-weight numerical parity is still not persisted, and the TorchTitan production block still needs its HC parity gate.

production_sparse_mla_reference_gate.reason: Reference backend passes ABI smoke but intentionally fails S4096 production preflight; fused long-sequence sparse MLA/LI remains the needed production path.

moe_training_semantics_gate.reason: EP routed backward and MORI+AITER bridge smokes pass exact/near exact gradients on small shapes, but final fused MoE backward is still pending.

next_actions: Keep the first-class Flash preset aligned with the HF config contract artifact.
Use the persisted MI350 S=1 FP32/BF16 local oracle as a smoke gate, then extend the reference harness toward matched HF numerical parity.

Use the new local Hyper-Connection full-layer oracle as the clean reference smoke, then build or record an HF numerical parity harness for one Flash layer with matched weights and inputs; until then, do not claim HF validation.
Keep current 4x/8x canary perf reporting separate from final fused sparse MLA/LI and fused MoE backward readiness.
bottom_line: The reference story is usable for local development but not complete against the original reference-first goal. We have an HF Flash config contract, a matching local Flash preset, an MI350 full Flash-dimension S=1 FP32/BF16 local layer-oracle smoke, local PyTorch autograd/parity smokes, MoE training semantics smokes, a local Hyper-Connection primitive smoke, a local HC full-layer Flash smoke, and a source-contract preflight showing the remaining HF/TorchTitan adapter work; we do not yet have persisted HF numerical full-layer parity or S4096 reference coverage.
